page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V45 cores with multithreading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, the x86_64 processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core, and includes a 945 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10% tier. However, multi-threaded scaling shows some limitations; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth remains average, measuring 460.91 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 48.62 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-model LLM inference is a bottleneck, with 7B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%, though smaller 135M model inference and Redis SET operations perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-4ads_v7 offers a high density of memory and local storage relative to its 4 vCPUs, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The combination of 128.0 GB of RAM, a 945 GB local disk, and strong single-core performance makes this server highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and data-intensive applications with moderate parallel processing requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 128.0 GB of RAM and a 945 GB local storage disk. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests placing in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded scaling is average, and large-model LLM prompt processing is poor. This resource profile offers high memory density relative to compute cores, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand extensive multi-core parallelization. It is highly suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and moderate data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A high-memory virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and local storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for relational databases and in-memory caching layers
